title: System Recovery Time Objective Tracking from DR Drills

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
index=grc sourcetype=dr:drill earliest=-365d
| eval rto_min=recovery_end_epoch - failover_start_epoch
| lookup app_tiers.csv application OUTPUT rto_target_min
| where rto_min > rto_target_min
| table drill_id, application, rto_min, rto_target_min, notes
value: Captures measured recovery time metrics from DR drills for CP-10 system recovery and reconstitution.
implementation: (1) Ensure epoch fields populated at drill completion; (2) store raw drill packets in secure share; (3) feed POA&M if missed; (4) integrate with IR-8 updates.
visualization: Table (RTO breaches), Bar chart (by app), Single value (worst RTO)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
